title: Thermal Ecology in R
text:
Welcome
This book is a practical guide for working with data related to the field of thermal ecology using R. It focuses specifically on the processes and challenges you’ll face with such data, and provides best practices for reporting.

title: Thermal Ecology in R
text:
1. Environmental Data
Learn how to load and clean raw data from own data loggers, access climate databases and microclimate simulations, and calculating metrics of heatwaves.

Data loggers
Online climatology data
Online microclimate data
Detecting heatwaves/cold spells

title: Thermal Ecology in R
text:
2. Physiological Data

Thermal performance curves
Respirometry
Thermal-death-time curves
Q10s and rates of change

title: Thermal Ecology in R
text:
3. Behavioural Data

Torpor/arousal patterns
Thermal images
Thermoregulation

title: Thermal Ecology in R
text:
4. Comparative Data

Effect sizes
Comparative analysis
Scaling relationships

title: Thermal Ecology in R
text:
5. Simulation

Operative temeprature models
Dynamic Energy Budget

title: Thermal Ecology in R
text:
Acknowledgements
This book was inspired by the Cleaning Biodiversity Data in R book. This book is a collaborative effort by members of the Thermal Ecology Alliance.

title: 1  Introduction
text:
This is a book created from markdown and executable code.
See Knuth (1984) for additional discussion of literate programming.
